page: Amazon Web Services m7g.8xlarge is a general-purpose server instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor and custom AWS Graviton processors. It features 32 dedicated physical cores utilizing the arm64 architecture, specifically the AWS Neoverse-V1 CPU family running at 2.6 GHz. The processor lacks hyperthreading, allocating exactly one thread per physical core. It is equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. The instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 15 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates strong memory performance, with the PassMark memory score ranking in the top tier (top 10%). Large-block read-only and write memory bandwidth tests also show strong results, reaching 237.87 GB/sec and 109.3 GB/sec respectively. Multi-core performance is robust, highlighted by a strong Geekbench multi-core score of 17678.0 and a strong PassMark CPU physics test result. However, single-core metrics, such as the Geekbench single-core score of 1535.0, and standard integer operations generally fall within the average range. Large-model LLM inference text generation and prompt processing represent a performance bottleneck, scoring in the bottom 10%.

Given its custom ARM-based architecture and dedicated core allocation, the m7g.8xlarge offers a resource-dense profile that is highly cost-efficient for multi-threaded workloads compared to traditional x86 alternatives. The lack of bundled local storage means storage-heavy workloads must rely on external network-attached volumes. This instance is well-suited for general-purpose application hosting, static web serving (which demonstrated strong throughput of 49.88 GB/sec), software compilation, database operations, and multi-threaded data processing tasks that benefit from high memory bandwidth and dedicated physical cores.
description: Amazon Web Services m7g.8xlarge is a general-purpose server instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor and custom AWS Graviton processors. Built on the arm64 architecture, it features 32 dedicated physical cores utilizing the AWS Neoverse-V1 CPU family running at 2.6 GHz, with 128.0 GB of system memory (4.0 GB per core). The instance lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs, but provides a baseline network bandwidth of 15 Gbps. Benchmarks show top-tier memory performance, with strong large-block memory bandwidth and robust multi-core processing capabilities, though single-core and LLM inference metrics remain average to poor. This resource-dense, ARM-based configuration is highly efficient for multi-threaded applications, static web serving, software compilation, and database operations.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m7g.8xlarge is an arm64 general-purpose instance featuring 32 dedicated Graviton physical cores, 128 GB RAM, and 15 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier memory performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m7g.8xlarge is an arm64 general-purpose instance with 32 dedicated Graviton cores, 128 GB RAM, and top-tier memory performance.
tagline: An arm64 general-purpose instance featuring dedicated physical cores and top-tier memory performance for multi-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by AWS Graviton Neoverse-V1 processors with 32 dedicated physical cores.
Equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory, delivering top-tier memory benchmark scores.
Provides 15 Gbps baseline network bandwidth without local storage or GPUs.
Strong multi-core performance and high-throughput static web serving capabilities.
Optimized for database operations, software compilation, and general-purpose applications.
